description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 unit/build/flake/data cluster: test-data design vs product data-layer design (multi-tenant-data-architect), security-fixture test suites (multi-tenant-security-tester), automation structure (qa-automation-architect), and data-caused flakes (flaky-test-detective).

cases.prompt: Our tests share one seeded workspace and collide in parallel — design deterministic, isolated fixtures and personas for all suites.
cases.reason: Fixture/persona/isolation design is test-data architecture — the data itself, not the framework layout, and prevention rather than a single flake case.

cases.prompt: Choose between shared tables with tenant keys and schema-per-tenant for our product database.
cases.reason: That is the product's tenant data layer; test data mirrors whatever it decides.

cases.prompt: Build the two-tenant fixture suite that proves cross-tenant reads are denied on every endpoint.
cases.reason: The A/B negative-test suite (fixtures + denial assertions) is owned by the shipped security tester; this skill only keeps general catalogs compatible with it.

cases.prompt: One integration test fails intermittently with a unique-constraint violation — diagnose it.
cases.reason: A specific intermittent failure is flake casework (which may then cite shared-data design as the cause and hand systemic fixes here).
